codeReview.summary: The fix correctly relaxes the pending cadence assertion (>=2 polls) and Enter budget assertion (<=4), but the new runtime mouse-event check can silently miss real mouse events, so the original blocking no-click requirement is not actually guaranteed.

issues.description: The runtime-event parser looks for raw SGR mouse bytes via `\x1b\[<...` (`MOUSE_EVENT_PATTERN`), but the harness writes `runtime-input.log` via `script -kq` (client/tests/tuistory/bin/run_scenario.sh:110), which records escapes in caret notation (`^[`) rather than raw 0x1b bytes. Repro: `printf '\033[<0;10;20M\033[<0;10;20m' | script -kq /tmp/script-keys-proof.log cat` yields `^[[<0;10;20M^[[<0;10;20m` (hex `5e5b5b3c...`). This means `runtime_mouse_events(...)` can return `[]` even when mouse-down/up events were emitted, so the prior blocking requirement from `scenario-nav-keyboard-escape` remains unresolved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill requires event-log validation for keyboard-only flows, but does not document the `script -k` log encoding format (`^[` escapes), which made it easy to implement a raw-escape regex that cannot detect logged mouse events.

summary: I reviewed the fix feature transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit diff, plus both original failed feature diffs and prior review reports. The cadence and Enter-budget failures are fixed, but the runtime no-click verification is still unsound because mouse events in `script -k` logs are not parsed by the current regex, so this fix review fails.
